findings.title: WorkerRegistry.loadFromDisk() silently swallows config load errors
findings.detail: In workers.ts loadFromDisk(), the catch block sets workers to an empty array with no log output. A typo or missing config/workers.json causes the daemon to boot with zero workers and block all work, with no operator-visible warning explaining why.
findings.fix_sketch: Add console.warn in the catch block that includes the error message and configPath so operators see the failure in logs.

findings.title: Discord channel broadcast errors silently swallowed in markCompleted
findings.detail: In queue/sources/discord.ts line 151, postChannelMessage is called with .catch(() => {}) which discards all errors without logging. If Discord is unreachable, the operator never learns the channel ping was skipped and onPostFailed is not invoked.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace the empty catch with a catch that logs via console.warn and calls onPostFailed callback so errors surface in observability.

findings.title: Git push failures in onForcePr proceed unconditionally to PR creation
findings.detail: In daemon.ts onForcePr, if the git push fails (e.g. permissions, worktree gone), the code logs a warning and falls through to pulls.create. This causes a confusing '422 branch not found' error from GitHub that masks the real push failure. Non-recoverable push errors should short-circuit the PR creation.
findings.fix_sketch: Inspect the push error message; if it does not match 'already up-to-date' or 'Everything up-to-date', return early before calling pulls.create.

findings.title: unifiedToSprintId lookup may return undefined but sprintId used without guard in audit event
findings.detail: In daemon.ts onForcePr, sprintId is assigned from unifiedToSprintId.get(taskId) at line 284 and only used in the if(sprintId) block. However, the undefined path falls through to the else branch which correctly handles the legacy path. The audit event in the else branch captures tk.sprintId directly, so the logic is correct, but the variable is narrowly typed and the intent is non-obvious to future readers.
findings.fix_sketch: Annotate the code with a comment explaining that sprintId is only used within its guarded block. No behavioral change needed.

findings.title: WorkerRegistry loadFromDisk watcher catch block has no comment
findings.detail: In workers.ts attachWatcher(), the catch block swallowing the watcher creation failure has a helpful comment but the loadFromDisk catch is bare. Keeping both consistent aids readability.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a comment to the loadFromDisk catch or consolidate with the fix for AUDIT-IFleet-a1b2c3d4.
